acceptance_conditions.condition: protocol, route identities, task builder, and source hashes remain exact
24 transfer groups produce 192 three-arm rows and 576 executions
all transferred and target-specific compiled routes retain semantic fidelity at least 0.9999999999
portfolio transfer-automatic mean is at least -0.005 and bootstrap lower is at least -0.01
portfolio transfer-target mean is at least -0.005 and bootstrap lower is at least -0.01
at least 20 of 24 groups have mean transfer-target delta at least -0.02
zero rows have transfer-target regression below -0.05
each target snapshot has mean transfer-target delta at least -0.01
challenge commitment, hidden seeds, row hashes, reveal, and transcript replay
temporal calibration, cross-machine, hardware, advantage, BQP, solved-frontier, and credit claims remain false

challenge_protocol.automatic_rule: fresh optimization-level-3 compile on target snapshot with hidden transpiler seed
challenge_protocol.shared_seed_rule: all three arms in one row share one simulator seed
challenge_protocol.target_rule: recompile target R143 mapping, policy_id, and realization_seed on target snapshot
challenge_protocol.transfer_rule: carry source R143 mapping, policy_id, and realization_seed; recompile on target snapshot

claim_boundary.what_is_not_supported_even_if_accepted: temporal same-device calibration transfer, cross-machine transfer, real hardware, mitigation, protocol soundness, quantum advantage, BQP separation, or solved B4/B8/B10

phase_protocol: commit a fresh secret after public preregistration
derive hidden transpiler, simulator, and bootstrap seeds
compile both frozen route identities on each target snapshot
execute all three arms with one shared simulator seed per row
reveal and verify the acceptance transcript
research_question: Can every R143 route transfer to both other fake-backend calibration snapshots without material portfolio or target-specific regression?
